titleStmt.title: SII 28.50: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 50. (A.R. No. 49 of 1913.) SIVĀYAM, KULITTALAI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT.

summary.p: Śivapurīśvara Temple, second gōpuram, left and right of entrance.

p.hi: Tribhuvanachakravartti Kulōttugga-chōḻa (III) Year 4, Dhanus, śu. 3, Thursday, Tiruvōṇam, Irregular.
p.note: The details of date may correspond to 1252 A.D., December 5, f.d.n. 29. Then it will have to be understood that the regnal year inscribed is a mistake for 6. However L.D. Pillai equates the date to 1181 A.D., December 10 in the reign of Vīra-Rājēndra Kulōttugga-Chōḷa (A.R.Ep., 1914, p. 59).

summary.p: This inscription erased in portions, records that mahā-pradhāni maṇḍalika [.]rāri Aḷiya-chōmayadaṇḍa-nāyakar, Chēvaya-daṇḍanāyakkar, Māvantan Sōmanāthaviṭṭaiyar of the araimaṉai-kūchar of Sōmīśvaradēvar (the Hoysala king ?). Śrī-rudra-mahēśvaras, sthānattār and nagaram of Śivapāda-śēkharapuram (met and) examined the accounts/affairs (tiru-chchelavu) of the temple of Tirumāṇikkamalaiuḍaiya-nāyaṉār in Kuṟukkai-nāḍu in Irājagambhīra-vaḷanāḍu and decided about tiṭṭu and tanichu pertaining to the temple.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī.
tiripuvaṉa
cakkaravatti
kaḷ Irāce

ttu tirutiyai

maiyum peṟṟa

tu nāḷ Irā
ca kempira va

ṟukkai nāṭ

stiśrīmaṉumakā prā

rāri Aḷiya comaya

cevaya teṇṭa
nāyakkarum co
miśvaratevar Arai

nāta viṭṭaiyaru
m śrīrudraśrīmāhe
śvararum sthānatta
